is a city on the [Bight of Benin](/wiki/Bight_of_Benin "Bight of Benin") and [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") coast of [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana"). It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) east of the capital city; [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"), in the region of [Greater Accra](/wiki/Greater_Accra_Region "Greater Accra Region"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and is the capital of the [Tema Metropolitan District](/wiki/Tema_Metropolis_District "Tema Metropolis District"). As of 2013, Tema is the eleventh most populous settlement in Ghana, with a population of approximately 161,612 people – a marked decrease from its 2005 figure of 209,000.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-GW-4) The [Greenwich Meridian](/wiki/Greenwich_Meridian "Greenwich Meridian") (00 [Longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude")) passes directly through the city.[[5]](#cite_note-GHD-5) It also had [the Meridian Rock](/wiki/The_Meridian_Rock "The Meridian Rock"). Tema is locally nicknamed the "Harbour City" because of its status as Ghana's largest [seaport](/wiki/Seaport "Seaport"). It consists of 25 different communities which are numbered accordingly with each of them having easy access to the basic amenities.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

**Tema** is a city on the [Bight of Benin](/wiki/Bight_of_Benin "Bight of Benin") and [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") coast of [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana"). It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) east of the capital city; [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"), in the region of [Greater Accra](/wiki/Greater_Accra_Region "Greater Accra Region"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and is the capital of the [Tema Metropolitan District](/wiki/Tema_Metropolis_District "Tema Metropolis District"). As of 2013, Tema is the eleventh most populous settlement in Ghana, with a population of approximately 161,612 people – a marked decrease from its 2005 figure of 209,000.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-GW-4) The [Greenwich Meridian](/wiki/Greenwich_Meridian "Greenwich Meridian") (00 [Longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude")) passes directly through the city.[[5]](#cite_note-GHD-5) It also had [the Meridian Rock](/wiki/The_Meridian_Rock "The Meridian Rock"). Tema is locally nicknamed the "Harbour City" because of its status as Ghana's largest [seaport](/wiki/Seaport "Seaport"). It consists of 25 different communities which are numbered accordingly with each of them having easy access to the basic amenities.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Tema is a city constructed on the site of a small fishing village.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Tema was commissioned by Ghana's first president, [Kwame Nkrumah](/wiki/Kwame_Nkrumah "Kwame Nkrumah"), and grew rapidly after the construction of a large harbor in 1961. The Tema [metropolis](/wiki/Metropolis "Metropolis") was designed, planned and developed by the award-winning [urban planner](/wiki/Urban_planner "Urban planner") and Ghana's first [architect](/wiki/Architect "Architect"), [Theodore S. Clerk](/wiki/Theodore_S._Clerk "Theodore S. Clerk").[[8]](#cite_note-8) The design team included a number of architects trained in [London](/wiki/London "London") at the [Architectural Association](/wiki/Architectural_Association_School_of_Architecture "Architectural Association School of Architecture").[[9]](#cite_note-9) It is now a major trading center, home to an [oil refinery](/wiki/Oil_refinery "Oil refinery") and numerous factories, and is linked to Accra by a highway and railway. Tema is one of Ghana's two deep seaports, the other being [Sekondi-Takoradi](/wiki/Sekondi-Takoradi "Sekondi-Takoradi").[[5]](#cite_note-GHD-5) Tema became an Autonomous Council in 1974 and was elevated to the status of a Metropolitan Assembly in December 1990. Tema metropolitan forms part of the sixteen Metropolis, Municipalities and Districts in the [Greater Accra Region](/wiki/Greater_Accra_Region "Greater Accra Region"). The Metropolitan shares boundaries with [Ashaiman Municipal](/wiki/Ashaiman_Municipal "Ashaiman Municipal"), [Adenta Municipal District](/wiki/Adenta_Municipal_District "Adenta Municipal District"), and [Ledzokuku-Krowor Municipal District](/wiki/Ledzokuku-Krowor_Municipal_District "Ledzokuku-Krowor Municipal District") to the west respectively, to the east with [Kpone Katamanso District](/wiki/Kpone_Katamanso_District "Kpone Katamanso District"), to the North with [Dangme West District](/wiki/Shai_Osudoku_District "Shai Osudoku District") and to the South with the Gulf of Guinea.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Tema was built on the site of a small fishing village called Torman, named for the local name of the [calabash](/wiki/Calabash "Calabash") plant, Tor, which was cultivated there. "Tema" is derived from a corruption of "Torman". The government identified the site before independence, and in 1952 acquired 166 square kilometres (64 mi2) of land north of the harbor, which was entrusted to the [Tema Development Corporation](/wiki/Tema_Development_Corporation "Tema Development Corporation") for the new industrial and residential development. The villagers of Torman migrated to a new fishing ground around 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) away, which they called Newtown.[[10]](#cite_note-ghanaweb.com-10)

The main Tema Township was constructed, and the [Tema Harbour](/wiki/Tema_Harbour "Tema Harbour") officially opened, in 1962. Over the following decades, Tema grew into the industrial hub of Ghana, with a carefully constructed road layout featuring landscaping and street lights. It boasted modern recreational centres and other social amenities rare among African cities at the time.[[10]](#cite_note-ghanaweb.com-10) President Nkrumah appointed Theophilus Asiaw Mills as the first District Commissioner. The importance of Tema as a port and industrial hub is reflected by the fact that the [Ghana Police Service](/wiki/Ghana_Police_Service "Ghana Police Service") maintains a special policing region devoted entirely to the city.

A large population influx began in the 1960s owing to the town's employment opportunities, but the Tema Development Corporation was unable to construct housing and provide other services to meet the needs of the migrants.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11) The Tema Newtown district was overwhelmed by the sudden population growth, and became the poor cousin of Tema Township, receiving none of the latter's improved housing, geometrically laid roads, or social amenities. Moreover, royalties paid by Tema Newtown's companies to evicted villagers have not been used due to a chieftaincy dispute. The area's fishing potential was thus not fully exploited.[[10]](#cite_note-ghanaweb.com-10)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Climate")]

Tema is characterised by a [hot semi-arid climate](/wiki/Hot_semi-arid_climate "Hot semi-arid climate") (*BSh* under the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification")). It lies in the driest part of southern Ghana, experiencing average annual rainfall of about 750 millimetres (30 in). Average temperatures are very warm to hot year-round, typically exceeding 28 °C or 82.4 °F every day of the year, whilst minima never typically fall under 23 °C or 73.4 °F.[[5]](#cite_note-GHD-5)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

### Industry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Industry")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f1/Tema_Central_Mall.jpg/250px-Tema_Central_Mall.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tema_Central_Mall.jpg)

Tema Central Shopping Mall

The town's chief industrial products include aluminium, steel, processed fish, refined petroleum, textile, chemicals, food products, and cement.[[4]](#cite_note-GW-4) Major companies operating in Tema include [Volta Aluminium](/wiki/Volta_Aluminum_Company "Volta Aluminum Company") (VALCO), [Tema Oil Refinery](/wiki/Tema_Oil_Refinery "Tema Oil Refinery") (TOR), [Nestlé Ghana Ltd.](/wiki/Nestl%C3%A9 "Nestlé"), Wahome Steel Ltd, [Tema Shipyard](/wiki/Tema_Shipyard "Tema Shipyard").[[13]](#cite_note-directory-13) There is also a [free zone](/wiki/Free_economic_zone "Free economic zone") enclave in Tema.

### Seaport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Seaport")]

Main article: [Tema Harbour](/wiki/Tema_Harbour "Tema Harbour")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Containerterminal_in_Tema%2C_Ghana.jpg/250px-Containerterminal_in_Tema%2C_Ghana.jpg)](/wiki/File:Containerterminal_in_Tema,_Ghana.jpg)

A container terminal at Tema Port in 2008

Tema Port, which was opened in 1962, is the biggest of the two seaports in Ghana. In 2020 a $1.5 billion expansion project to increase container output to 3 million [TEU](/wiki/Twenty-foot_equivalent_unit "Twenty-foot equivalent unit") was completed. The port is currently one of the largest container ports in Africa.[[14]](#cite_note-14) It has a water-enclosed area of 1.7 square kilometres (0.66 mi2) and a total land area of 3.9 square kilometres (1.5 mi2). Apart from handling Ghanaian imports and exports, it is also a traffic junction, dealing with transit cargo destined for the landlocked countries of [Burkina Faso](/wiki/Burkina_Faso "Burkina Faso"), [Mali](/wiki/Mali "Mali") and [Niger](/wiki/Niger "Niger"). The port of Tema handles 80% of Ghana's import and export cargo, including the country's chief export, [cacao](/wiki/Cocoa_bean "Cocoa bean").

The port has 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) of [breakwaters](/wiki/Breakwaters "Breakwaters"), 12 deepwater berths, an outsize [oil tanker](/wiki/Oil_tanker "Oil tanker") berth, a dockyard, warehouses, and transit sheds. The port has open and covered areas for the storage of cargo, including a 77,200 m2 (7.7 hectares) paved area for the storage of containers, steel products and other conventional cargo. The port's container yard is capable of holding over 8,000 TEUs at any given time. The closed storage area, which is about 25,049 m2 (2.5 hectares) in area, consists of six sheds with a total storage capacity of 50,000 tonnes of cargo. The port also includes a 100,000 [dwt](/wiki/Deadweight_tonnage "Deadweight tonnage") dry dock and slipway facility.[[15]](#cite_note-GPHA-15) The harbour is operated by the [Ghana Ports and Harbours Authority](/wiki/Ghana_Ports_and_Harbours_Authority "Ghana Ports and Harbours Authority").

### Fishing harbor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Fishing harbor")]

Ghana has a long history of fishing. The Tema fishing harbour is at the eastern end of the town's commercial harbour. It comprises the Inner Fishing Harbour, the [Canoe](/wiki/Canoe "Canoe") Basin, the Outer Fishing Harbour, and a commercial area with marketing and cold storage facilities.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11)

The Inner Fishing Harbour was constructed along the Tema Main Port in 1962 to provide a handling facility for semi-industrial and industrial fishing vessels, and to encourage the development of the local [fishing industry](/wiki/Fishing_industry "Fishing industry"). In 1965, the Outer Fishing Harbour was constructed for bigger industrial vessels such as [trawlers](/wiki/Fishing_trawler "Fishing trawler"), [tuna](/wiki/Tuna "Tuna") vessels, and deep-sea carriers.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11) The trawlers operating in the area are 30–45 m (98–148 ft) long and are able to land 55–65 tonnes of fish (usually [redfish](/wiki/Redfish "Redfish") and club and scad [mackerel](/wiki/Mackerel "Mackerel")) per fishing trip. The tuna vessels range from smaller boats around 45–50 m (148–164 ft) in length, able to land 200–250 tonnes of catch, to larger versions with a length of 50–65 m (164–213 ft) and the ability to land up to 650 tonnes per fishing trip.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11) The largest fishing vessels, the deep-sea carriers (with lengths of 90–105 m, 295–344 ft), are mostly [chartered](/wiki/Chartering_(shipping) "Chartering (shipping)") vessels.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11) Since 1984, the national catch has averaged about 200,000 to 300,000 metric tonnes per annum. Ghana's tuna catch has maintained a stable level of 30,000 tonnes per annum since 1981.[[11]](#cite_note-OTAL-11)

The Canoe Basin caters for the [artisanal](/wiki/Artisanal "Artisanal") [fishermen](/wiki/Fishermen "Fishermen"). The basin is normally occupied by about 400 [canoes](/wiki/Canoe "Canoe"). These are mainly two types: wooden vessels, locally called "Legelege", and metallic vessels. The wooden canoes have lengths over all ([LOA](/wiki/Length_overall "Length overall")) ranging between 30 and 70 m (98 and 230 ft), mainly owned and operated by [indigenous](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples "Indigenous peoples") Ghanaians.[[15]](#cite_note-GPHA-15) Their operations peak during the months of June–September. Artisanal canoe fishing is responsible for about 70% of the catch.[[15]](#cite_note-GPHA-15)

### Development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Development")]

The Tema Development Corporation (TDC) is a [public entity](/wiki/Public_entity "Public entity") tasked with the planning and [development](/wiki/Rural_development "Rural development") of the entire city of Tema.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) [Affordable public housing](/wiki/Affordable_housing "Affordable housing") was developed by the government in conjunction with TDC and the State Housing Corporation.[[18]](#cite_note-HusainFaruqee1994-18) The corporation has been instrumental in developing the [harbour area of Tema](/wiki/Tema_Harbour "Tema Harbour") in particular, with modern housing.[[19]](#cite_note-Acquaah-Harrison2004-19) The corporation was set up in 1952 with the sole aim to develop and manage the township of Tema.[[20]](#cite_note-20) In 1963, the *Tema Development Corporation Act* was passed.[[21]](#cite_note-Justice1964-21) In recent years the corporation has had much investment from [Korea](/wiki/Korea "Korea"), including plans, as of 2013, to build a new stadium, an idea which proved unpopular with squatters.[[22]](#cite_note-Obeng-Odoom2013-22) The first chief executive officer of the corporation was [Theodore S. Clerk](/wiki/Theodore_S._Clerk "Theodore S. Clerk"), the first Ghanaian architect and award-winning [urban planner](/wiki/Urban_planner "Urban planner") who served in the [CEO](/wiki/Chief_executive_officer "Chief executive officer") position from 1963 after the enactment of the statute, until 1965.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Transportation")]

There are Public Transports from Tema to major cities such as [Kumasi](/wiki/Kumasi "Kumasi"); [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"); [Mim, Ahafo](/wiki/Mim,_Ahafo "Mim, Ahafo") ; [Cape Coast](/wiki/Cape_Coast "Cape Coast"), [Sunyani](/wiki/Sunyani "Sunyani"); [Takoradi](/wiki/Takoradi "Takoradi"); [Tamale](/wiki/Tamale,_Ghana "Tamale, Ghana"); [Ho](/wiki/Ho,_Ghana "Ho, Ghana"); [Wa](/wiki/Wa,_Ghana "Wa, Ghana"); [Bolgatanga](/wiki/Bolgatanga "Bolgatanga"); [Elubo](/wiki/Elubo "Elubo"); [Aflao](/wiki/Aflao "Aflao"), [Techiman](/wiki/Techiman "Techiman").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tema&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Education")]

[SOS-Hermann Gmeiner International College](/wiki/SOS-Hermann_Gmeiner_International_College "SOS-Hermann Gmeiner International College") (SOS-HGIC), a private mixed [boarding school](/wiki/Boarding_school "Boarding school") catering to the 10th through 13th grades, is located in Tema. It previously used the [International General Certificate of Secondary Education](/wiki/International_General_Certificate_of_Secondary_Education "International General Certificate of Secondary Education") (IGCSE) for the 10th and 11th grades and the [International Baccalaureate](/wiki/International_Baccalaureate "International Baccalaureate") (IB) for the 12th and 13th grades, but currently runs the full IB Diploma program for all four grades. The school was headed by Margaret Nkrumah for over 15 years, and is now headed by Mr Israel Titi Ofei and Nii Amaa Akita.

Tema also has an [international school](/wiki/International_school "International school"), [Tema International School](/wiki/Tema_International_School "Tema International School") (TIS), which is second to HGIC, and a senior high school, [Tema Secondary School](/w/index.php?title=Tema_Senior_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tema Senior High School (page does not exist)") (TSS or Temasco), which was built on 22 September 1961. Tema has a number of public secondary schools such as [Chemu Senior High School](/wiki/Chemu_Senior_High_School "Chemu Senior High School") in [Community 4](/wiki/Tema_Community_4 "Tema Community 4"), [Tema Methodist Day School](/w/index.php?title=Tema_Methodist_Day_Senior_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tema Methodist Day Senior High School (page does not exist)"), Mahean Senior High School, [Our Lady of Mercy Senior High School](/w/index.php?title=Our_Lady_of_Mercy_Senior_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Our Lady of Mercy Senior High School (page does not exist)") and Tema Technical Institute.

Private preparatory schools include Roberts Memorial School, Creator Schools, St Paul Methodist Primary and JHS, Marbs Preparatory School, Datus Complex, Deks Educational Institute, Naylor SDA School, Tema Christian Centre, Tema Parents Association, First Baptist School, Tema Regular Baptist School, Queen Esther School, Dorsons School, Adwenie Memorial, Creator School, New Covenant School, St Alban's School, Lorenz Wolf School, Bexhill School Complex, Life International School, Mazon Grace Academy, Santabarbera School, Angels Specialist School, First Star Academy, Pentecost School, Star School Complex, Tema Ridge, St John Bosco School and Rosharon Montessori School.

Public primary and junior high schools in Tema include Twedaase Primary School, Star School, Aggrey Road School, Republic Road School, Padmore School, Mante Din Drive, Amen Basic, Manhean SDA School, and Bethel Methodist School.
